feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Build App request destination 401
entry.content:
Hi Experts:

I create a SAP Build APP, but trigger process failed for 401

in preview will return 401 to 

{
"status": 401,
"statusText": "Unauthorized",
"headers": {
"cache-control": "no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
"expires": "0",
"pragma": "no-cache",
"referrer-policy": "no-referrer",
"www-authenticate": "Bearer error=\"invalid_token\", error_description=\"Error initializing JWT decoder: The token is invalid: Jwt token with audience [destination-xsappname!b9.instance, sb-cloneb3a595c52ed142ec86069eecbc4be8b3!b7280|destination-xsappname!b9, uaa, openid, xs_account, destination-xsappname!b9.subaccount, destination-xsappname!b9] is not issued for these clientIds: [sb-xsuaa!b3591, xsuaa!b3591].\", error_uri=\"https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-3.1\"",
"x-content-type-options": "nosniff",
"x-frame-options": "DENY",
"x-vcap-request-id": "c09776e6-8d3a-4d72-73ec-24a760e9b37b",
"x-xss-protection": "0",
"date": "Fri, 30 Jan 2026 06:45:24 GMT",
"strict-transport-security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload;",
"connection": "close"
},
"responseText": ""
}
my destination is

in postman, use token get from the clientId and secret in destination, I can call my process successfully

is there someone can help me, thanks a lot!

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

it can work now, maybe it's because there's some delay with the server.

SAP Build SAP Build Apps SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Is SAP Build subscription alone sufficient to create mobile applications, or is SAP Build Apps subsc
entry.content:
As per SAP documentation, SAP Build is a bundle that includes the following services:

  1. SAP Build Apps

  2. SAP Build Process Automation

  3. SAP Build Work Zone

I would like to clarify the licensing and subscription aspect.

If we subscribe only to SAP Build, is that sufficient to create and deploy mobile applications using SAP Build Apps?

Or do we need to purchase/subscribe to SAP Build Apps separately, even though it is part of the SAP Build bundle?

It would be helpful if someone could explain:

Whether SAP Build Apps is automatically included with SAP Build

Or if it requires an individual subscription/entitlement

Any limitations (users, runtime, mobile deployment) with SAP Build Apps under SAP Build

Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I'm working on SAP Build Process Automation, here I'm working on document information extraction and making use of a document template, I have created data types and also extracted invoice data. 

The next step is where I'm creating invoice variable, here when I'm clicking create custom data option, it is not working.

Attaching the screenshot for your reference. Kindly do the needful as soon as possible.

Thank you so much

Kind Regards

Tazeem R

entry.title: Business Partner creation $batch fails at “Call Web Service” in SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am using SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) together with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to execute SAP’s standard template “Mass Create of Business Partners (58Z)”.
My goal is to create Business Partners via the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER OData service using a $batch POST request.

However, the automation fails at the Call Web Service step with the following error:

irpa_core.error.RequestError: Response code 401 (Unauthorized)

The earlier steps run successfully; the issue occurs only when BPA executes the actual $batch POST for Business Partner creation.

Environment

  • Target system: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Main automation tool: SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
  • Scenario: Create Business Partners via API_BUSINESS_PARTNER $batch
  • Template:  Mass Maintenance of Business Partners (58Z)

 


Current problem

  • BPA reaches the Call Web Service activity that performs:
    POST https://<tenant>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/$batch
  • At this moment, BPA throws:
    401 Unauthorized (irpa_core.error.RequestError)

    Additional data I can provide (sanitized)

    • Communication User / System / Arrangement screenshots
    • BPA logs for HEAD and Call Web Service (request/response headers)
    • If needed, I can test in Postman using the same Communication User and share:
      • GET A_BusinessPartner?$top=1
      • HEAD x-csrf-token: fetch
      • POST $batch

    This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated.


This may be a basic question, but any guidance or clarification would be greatly appreciated. 

—Moon14

entry.title: SAP BUILD PROCESS AUTOMATION - Project not loading
entry.content:
I am currently learning from the SAP build automation course.

in the SAP BUILD lobby i can see my process automation; but when i am trying to open that process automation the next screen is not loading


How can i resolve this issue

entry.title: Clarification on SAP Build Apps Subscription Visibility under build-default Plan
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We need your clarification regarding SAP Build Apps subscription visibility in our SAP BTP subaccount.

Currently, our subaccount has the following subscriptions active under the build-default plan:

SAP Build Process Automation – build-default

SAP Business Application Studio – build-default

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition – build-default

As per SAP documentation and licensing understanding, SAP Build Apps is included within the build-default plan and does not appear as a standalone subscription.

However, when we attempt to create a project using Create Project → Web Application (low-code) in SAP Build Apps, the system displays the status as “Not Subscribed”, which creates confusion from an entitlement perspective.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether SAP Build Apps entitlement is correctly provided through the build-default plan in our subaccount, and

  2. Why the project creation wizard displays “Not Subscribed” despite the build-default plan being active.

Your confirmation will help us proceed and also communicate the correct information to our stakeholders.

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: How to get the approver when a form is send for approval to multiple recipients in SAP BPA Workflow
entry.content: In sap BPA workflow, I am sending for for approval form to multiple recipients. I want to get the the recipient who took the action made the form Approve/Rejected. Whoever took the action. So as to update the recipient details to System.

entry.title: How to access Environment Variables in Script Task/BPA workflow without using Automation in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working on a business process in SAP Build Process Automation and I am trying to access Environment Variables directly within a Script Task.

My Scenario:

  • I have defined Environment Variables (type: String) in the project settings.

  • My project consists of a Process (Workflow) with Script Task.

  • Important: I am not using an Automation (Desktop Agent) in this specific flow.

I have referred multiple blogs but could not find the solution anywhere.

entry.title: Unable to capture "Substitute" assignments for email notifications using standard features.
entry.content:
Component : SAP Build Process Automation - Workflow

Problem Description: We are observing a gap between substitute feature of My Inbox and task creation in the My Inbox application via BPA and the custom email notifications triggered by SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) workflows.

Technical Details
Feature in Use: Standard "Substitute" functionality within the My Inbox app.
Issue: When a user defines a substitute in My Inbox, the task is correctly redirected within the inbox UI. However, the custom BPA workflow remains unaware of this change.
Impact: Email notifications generated by the BPA workflow are still sent to the original approver rather than the active substitute.

Question for the Community:
Is there a standard or recommended way to make a BPA workflow aware of an active "My Inbox" substitute for the purpose of sending email notifications?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation Inputs no longer visible in Design Mode
entry.content:
Hello, 
Since today, when testing a Task Automation in SAP Build Studio, the mandatory input variables are no longer appearing in the launcher box. Was a new feature introduced or it's a temporary bug? Technical reference here Input/Output Parameters | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP GUI Automation Failing to Recognize Screen – Possible Impact of Windows Idle Session Timeout
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are currently experiencing an issue with one of our SAP GUI screen-based automations (SAP Build Process Automation / SAP GUI scripting).

The automation is able to:

  • Successfully log into Windows

  • Launch SAP GUI

  • Log into SAP

  • Navigate to the required transaction

  • Read data from SAP

However, after performing a period of background processing (Excel data handling), when the automation attempts to continue interacting with SAP GUI, it fails to recognize the expected screen.

At that moment, we observe the following popup message in SAP GUI:

“Idle timer expired. Session has been idle over its time limit. It will be disconnected in 2 minutes. Press any key now to continue session.”

Important observations:

  • SAP GUI does not immediately close.

  • The SAP Logon window remains open.

  • However, the automation is no longer able to recognize the screen elements.

  • The error typically appears as “Page not recognized” or “Element not found”.

We would like to confirm whether:

  1. A Windows Remote Desktop idle session timeout policy is active.

  2. Any recent security or infrastructure changes could be affecting GUI scripting sessions during periods of inactivity.

The automation is screen-based and depends on a stable SAP GUI session without modal popups or session interruptions.

Could you please confirm if the idle timeout policy (Windows or SAP level) may be impacting SAP GUI scripting and RPA execution?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation, My Processes App not working as intended
entry.content:
I am not sure if the My Processes App from Build Process Automation is functioning correctly in Shared Environments.

I was under the assumption that with this app it is possible for users with the ProcessAutomationParticipant role to look at the status of processes in which they have been involved in, as for example, approvers.

I am not able to view any processes in which I am involved in from shared environments that are not owned by me, unless I give myself the Supervise Authorization which leads to me being able to view all processes from that environment, not just the ones I was involved in.

Is this intended? What is the setup so users are only able to only see the processes in which they have been involved, that are running on a shared environment?

entry.title: Automation around Transports (STMS_IMPORT)
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am wondering whether somebody from community has "good" experience with automation transport requests in SAP. I am talking about technical transports requests (STMS), not logistics.

In the past I build a process automation with a form where selected users could enter a transport request. The bot checked that request for some preconditions and imported it then into a SAP system.

In the meantime and with much more experience I doubt whether this scenario is really good for a "bot". The check of the preconditions is really complex, because the request is only allowed to have special objects included and nothing else. I am importing with BAPI. There I only can start the import but don't get a reliable feedback (especially for huge requests). Also there are no BAPI for STMS_QA, so I don't get this list clean.

I also noticed that SAP itself doesn't have any automation around STMS_IMPORT in her portfolio (store). That must also have some reasons because there are over 600 projects included and STMS uses nearly every customer.

In the past we dealt that topic with tickets and a manual import from "IT guys".

What are your thoughts about that issue?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation (IRPA) compatibility with Windows Server 2025
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently evaluating an upgrade of our bot execution server to Windows Server 2025, and we would like to confirm the compatibility of SAP Build Process Automation (Desktop Agent / IRPA) with this operating system.

Our concern is whether running the bots on Windows Server 2025 could affect:

  1. SAP GUI scripting recognition
  2. Screen-based automation stability
  3. RDP session behavior
  4. Desktop Agent execution
  5. Session locking / auto logoff scenarios

Currently, the robots are running on Windows Server (previous version) without issues, but before upgrading we would like to confirm:

  1. Is Windows Server 2025 officially supported by SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent?
  2. Are there any known limitations or required configurations?
  3. Could there be impacts on SAP GUI automation or screen element recognition?

Any official documentation or SAP Notes references would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Using Concur API in Build Proces Automation
entry.content:
Hi, 

in SAP Build Process Automation we're trying to access Concur data via API. 

We tried to run GET Retrieve Users (Identity v4 API) and stated /profile/identity/v4 as Resource Path and received this message: 

"{\"status\":400,\"schemas\":[\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error\",\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\"],\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\":{\"messages\":[{\"code\":\"BAD_REQUEST\"}]},\"detail\":\"Company ID is required\"}"

We're assuming that there's something missing within our destination configuration in our BTP subaccount. 
We used the following settings:

Type: HTTP
Proxy Type: Internet
URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID
Client Secret
Token Service URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com/oauth2/v0/token
Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

Additional Properties
companyID


In Concur we generated an OAuth2 App with the Grants refresh_token, client_credentials and password and selected all available scopes.

Any hints on how to make this API work are greatly appreciated!

Thanks
Franziska

entry.title: Insufficient Privilege while creating agent environment in SAP Build Control Tower
entry.content:
Hello, 

As part of working on this tutorial on step 5, I want to create a new environment. I have assigned all the process automation roles - process automation admin, developer, expert etc. But still not able to create the environment and getting the error below.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-setup-desktop-3-0-agent.html

Can you please help.

Thanks,

Mainak

entry.title: Questions about the file attachment feature in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello,

This is my first post and I’m new to SAP Build Process Automation, so I apologize in advance for any omissions or mistakes.
I’ve just started working with SAP Build Process Automation and I’m trying to figure out how to attach files.

I have two questions:

  1. It seems that using a User Task might allow file attachments, but I’m unable to add a User Task to my process. If you have any ideas about what might be causing this, please let me know.
  2. If there is a simpler way to enable file attachments other than (1), I would appreciate your guidance. At the moment, I have limited ability to use other products such as SAP Build Work Zone.

Thank you in advance for your help.

 

>>>Dear Vennilla00,

Thank you very much for your response. If you happen to know, I would be grateful for your guidance.

I can’t seem to find a way to attach a file in my submit form. If you know the procedure or steps, I would greatly appreciate it if you could let me know.

entry.title: SAP Build process automation to Joule Deployment Fails
entry.content: I am trying to deploy a skill from SAP Build Process Automation to Joule, but the Integration Gateway rejects it with the error: 'Cannot use Joule formation connection, ias spba token missing in request header.' My System Landscape Formation is set up correctly, so I suspect this is a Trust Configuration or IAS user mapping issue preventing the required token from generating. Has anyone successfully resolved this specific authentication block during Joule deployments?

entry.title: 403 Error when calling an on-premise S/4 API from SAP Build Process Automation (SAP Desktop Agent).
entry.content:
We have a BTP Destination configured to point to a customer on prem S/4 system via Cloud Connector.

Our goal is to access S/4 APIs (e.g. API_PRODUCT_SRV) on this system from SAP Build Process Automation via SAP Build Actions.

I tested the action directly and these work fine, but when running the action from within a BPA Automation I get a 403 error.

Both the Action test and the BPA Automation action use the same BTP Destination.

Should this be possible and what are the steps I need to take?

Destination:

Authentication: BasicAuthentication
Proxy Type: OnPremise
URL: http://xxxxxxxxx/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_PRODUCT_SRV/
Additional Properties:
Key
sap.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
MobileEnabled
Value
true
Key
WebIDEEnabled
Value
true
Key
sap-client
Value
200
Key
WebIDEUsage
Value
odata_abap,dev_abap
Key
sap.build.usage
Value
odata_gen
Key
HTML5.DynamicDestination
Value
true
Key
HTML5.Timeout
Value
600000
Key
AppgyverEnabled
Value
true
 
Key
sap.build.processautomation.enabled
Value
true
Key
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled
Value
true
